summary: Elite Gaming Hub - A Free Fire tournament app with a dark theme featuring Neon Red and Black accents. Home screen includes a header with app name and profile icon, scrolling banner for Mega Tournaments, and match cards showing game mode, entry fee, prize pool, and a Join Now button. Clicking 'Join Now' navigates to a new 'Registration' page with input fields for In-Game Name and Player ID, and a 'Proceed to Payment' button.
architectural_notes: Dark theme with Neon Red and Black color accents throughout the app
Home screen layout: header → scrolling banner → match card list
Live Matches section uses static sample data (3 hardcoded tournament cards) instead of live backend fetch; connection error state is removed
Registration flow changed to page-based navigation: Join Now → /registration page (In-Game Name, Player ID fields + Proceed to Payment button) instead of modal

existing_features.title: Implement a dark theme with Neon Red (#FF0033 or similar vivid red) and black background accents applied consistently across the entire Home Screen. Typography should feel bold and gaming-oriented (e.g., uppercase headings, tight letter-spacing). Neon glow effects should be used on key interactive elements and highlights.

Create a fixed top header bar that displays the app name 'Elite Gaming Hub' on the left (styled with neon red accent) and a circular profile icon/avatar placeholder on the right. The header should have a dark background with a subtle neon red bottom border.

Add a horizontally auto-scrolling banner section labeled 'Mega Tournaments' below the header. The banner should contain at least 3 promotional slides with bold titles, neon red highlights, and a gaming visual style. The banner should auto-scroll on a timer and allow manual swipe/click navigation.

Below the banner, render a vertically scrollable list of match cards. Each card must display: game mode ('Free Fire Solo' or 'Free Fire Duo'), entry fee ('Entry Fee: ₹50'), prize pool ('Prize Pool: ₹500'), and a 'Join Now' button at the bottom of the card. Cards should have a dark card background with a neon red border/glow, and the 'Join Now' button should be styled in neon red.
Wire the frontend match card list to fetch data from the backend getMatches() query using React Query. Display a loading skeleton and an error state while data is being fetched.

Create a RegistrationModal component that opens when the 'Join Now' button on any MatchCard is clicked. The modal must collect three fields: 'In-Game Name', 'Player ID', and 'WhatsApp Number'. All fields are required. The modal must follow the existing dark neon-red theme (dark card background, neon red accents, Orbitron/Rajdhani fonts, neon glow on the submit button). A 'Next' or 'Proceed to Payment' button at the bottom submits the form and advances to the payment screen.

After the registration form is submitted, display a UPI Payment screen (as the next step in the same modal flow or a new modal). The screen must prominently show the UPI ID '[Apna UPI ID eithe likho]' with instructions to pay the entry fee via UPI. It must include an 'Upload Screenshot' button that allows the user to select and preview a payment screenshot image from their device. There must be no other payment gateway options (no Razorpay, Stripe, or similar integrations). The screen must follow the existing dark neon-red theme.

In the MatchList component, remove the error state UI (the 'Connection Failed' error display) and instead render 3 hardcoded static sample tournament cards when the backend query fails or is unavailable. Each static card must display: a game mode ('Free Fire Solo' or 'Free Fire Duo'), an entry fee ('Entry Fee: ₹50'), a prize pool ('Prize Pool: ₹500'), and a functional 'Join Now' button that opens the RegistrationModal flow. The static cards must use the same MatchCard component and styling as live data cards (dark card background, neon red border/glow, Orbitron/Rajdhani fonts).

Create a dedicated '/registration' route/page component (RegistrationPage) that displays two required input fields — 'In-Game Name' and 'Player ID' — and a 'Proceed to Payment' button. The page must receive the selected match context (game mode, entry fee, prize pool) via navigation state or URL params so it can display match details. The page must follow the existing dark neon-red gaming theme (dark background, neon red accents, Orbitron/Rajdhani fonts, neon glow on the button).

Update the 'Join Now' button in MatchCard to navigate to the '/registration' page (using React Router or equivalent client-side routing) instead of opening the existing RegistrationModal. Pass the match's game mode, entry fee, and prize pool as navigation state so the RegistrationPage can access them.

Set up client-side routing in the app (e.g., React Router) with at minimum two routes: '/' for the existing Home screen (Header + TournamentBanner + MatchList + footer) and '/registration' for the new RegistrationPage. Ensure the Header remains visible on the Registration page and a back navigation option (e.g., a back arrow or 'Back' link) is available to return to the Home screen.

Update the 'Join Now' button in MatchCard to navigate to the '/registration' page using client-side routing instead of opening the existing RegistrationModal. Pass the match's game mode, entry fee, and prize pool as navigation state so the RegistrationPage can access and display them.

In the MatchList component, remove the 'Connection Failed' error state UI entirely. When the backend query fails or is unavailable, render exactly 3 hardcoded static match cards with the following data: (1) Title: 'Bermuda Solo', gameMode: 'Free Fire Solo', entryFee: '₹50', prizePool: '₹500'; (2) Title: 'Bermuda Duo', gameMode: 'Free Fire Duo', entryFee: '₹50', prizePool: '₹500'; (3) Title: 'Purgatory Solo', gameMode: 'Free Fire Solo', entryFee: '₹50', prizePool: '₹500'. Each static card must use the existing MatchCard component with the same dark card background, neon red border/glow, Orbitron/Rajdhani fonts styling. The 'Join Now' button on each static card must store match context in sessionStorage and navigate to '/registration' exactly as live data cards do.

Update the RegistrationPage at '/registration' to include exactly two required input fields — 'In-Game Name' and 'Player ID' — along with a 'Proceed to Payment' button. The page must read match context (game mode, entry fee, prize pool) from sessionStorage (set by MatchCard on navigation) and display those details at the top of the form. All styling must follow the existing dark neon-red gaming theme (dark card background, neon red accents, Orbitron/Rajdhani fonts, neon glow on the button). A back-navigation option must be present to return to the Home screen.

new_feature_requests.summary: Home Screen with dark theme (Neon Red & Black), header with app name and profile icon
Scrolling banner for Mega Tournaments section
Match cards showing Free Fire Solo/Duo, Entry Fee ₹50, Prize Pool ₹500, and Join Now button

Store a sample list of match data in the Motoko backend as a stable array of records, each containing: matchId, gameMode (text), entryFee (Nat), prizePool (Nat), and status (text). Expose a query function getMatches() that returns all matches. Seed it with at least 4 example matches (2 Solo, 2 Duo).
After registration form, show a UPI payment screen with the user's UPI ID and an 'Upload Screenshot' button. Remove all default/other payment gateways.
Clicking 'Join Now' on a match card navigates to a new 'Registration' page with input fields for 'In-Game Name', 'Player ID', and a 'Proceed to Payment' button

Ensure that clicking the 'Join Now' button on any MatchCard (both live backend cards and the 3 static fallback cards in MatchList) stores the match's game mode, entry fee, and prize pool in sessionStorage and navigates to '/registration'. This must work consistently for all cards regardless of data source.
